rationale_for_baseline_tier: This is a flare of a KNOWN chronic dysautonomia (hyperadrenergic POTS) with a NEGATIVE acute red-flag screen: the heart rate normalises supine (~75), the patient is afebrile, denies chest pain and has had presyncope but no true syncope. The disturbance is strictly posture-dependent and relieved by lying flat. The alarming numbers (standing HR ~140, standing BP 155/100) reflect an orthostatic sympathetic surge, not a sustained emergency. The correct telehealth disposition is prompt in-person GP review within 2-3 days (for ECG and thyroid function to exclude the arrhythmic/endocrine mimics, and to consider a sympatholytic) PLUS firm red-flag safety-netting — mirroring the source note's own routine 2-3 day GP disposition. Emergency escalation (T4/T5) for the baseline stable flare would be over-triage. A FIRST presentation without a known autonomic diagnosis, or a positive red-flag screen, would shift the tier upward.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New severe or crushing central chest pain
escalation_edges.rationale: Chest pain superimposed on tachycardia raises acute coronary syndrome or an aortic emergency — a time-critical event requiring emergency ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get heavy or crushing pain in the centre of your chest, call 000 for an ambulance straight away and do not drive yourself.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Heart rate remains above ~130 bpm while lying completely flat for 20 minutes (loss of posture-dependency)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately if it does not settle within 20 minutes of lying flat
escalation_edges.rationale: A tachycardia that persists supine is no longer explained by posture and suggests a primary arrhythmia (SVT/AF) requiring emergency assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your heart keeps racing above about 130 even after lying flat and still for 20 minutes, call 000 — that is different from your usual pattern.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Actual fainting, collapse or loss of consciousness
escalation_edges.rationale: True syncope (as opposed to the current presyncope) raises the probability of a dangerous arrhythmia or structural cardiac cause and warrants emergency assessment, especially in someone living alone.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you actually black out or collapse, that needs an ambulance — call 000. Because you live alone, tell a friend or neighbour to check on you.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe headache, visual disturbance or focal weakness/numbness with the high standing blood pressure (end-organ features of a hypertensive emergency)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend an emergency department the same day (call 000 if severe or with chest pain)
escalation_edges.rationale: A hypertensive surge with new end-organ features (neurological or cardiac) would represent a hypertensive emergency needing urgent hospital assessment rather than routine GP review.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a severe headache, changes in your vision, or weakness or numbness on one side, go to an emergency department the same day — call 000 if it is severe.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Flare not settling with rest over 24 hours, unable to keep fluids down, worsening presyncope, reduced urine output or confusion
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Arrange a same-day GP or telehealth review
escalation_edges.rationale: Failure to settle, dehydration or worsening presyncope in a patient who lives alone warrants prompt same-day reassessment before the routine 2-3 day review.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If this isn't settling with rest over the next day, you can't keep fluids down, or you feel more faint, get a same-day GP or telehealth appointment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone, currently bedbound with severe presyncope and a high fall risk
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for arranging support: advise the patient to ask someone to come and stay with or check on her, not to stand unaided while symptomatic, and — if any emergency escalation edge is triggered — to call 000 rather than attempt to self-transport, given presyncope and no one present.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Right now, rest lying down and change position slowly, using the muscle-tensing technique before you stand. Cut out caffeine and stimulant drinks and keep sipping fluids. Book an in-person GP appointment within the next 2-3 days for a heart tracing (ECG) and thyroid tests, and take a log of your lying-versus-standing heart rate and blood pressure. Because you're on your own, ask someone to check on you today.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Book a GP appointment within 2-3 days; for after-hours advice call 13 HEALTH (13 43 25 84); call 000 for any of the emergency warning signs.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After hours: call 13 HEALTH (13 43 25 84) for triage advice, or attend an emergency department / call 000 if any red-flag warning sign occurs.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Take your home log of lying and standing heart rate and blood pressure, and note the timing and triggers of the episodes, to your GP appointment.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing the episode as benign anxiety/POTS with self-care only and NO emergency safety-net or medical review — misses a possible superimposed arrhythmia, hypertensive or thyrotoxic event and leaves a patient who lives alone without escalation advice.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The patient, alone, develops sustained supine tachycardia or chest pain overnight, believes she was told it is 'just POTS', delays calling 000, and collapses unwitnessed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine GP review in 1-2 weeks without firm red-flag safety-netting — too slow for the severity of the flare and the need to exclude the arrhythmic/endocrine mimics.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending a stable, posture-dependent flare of a known dysautonomia (negative acute red-flag screen, normal supine vitals) to ED or calling 000 — an unnecessary emergency presentation causing resource use and patient distress. This over-triage is the primary defect this case is built to detect.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot obtain an ECG, thyroid function or catecholamine testing, so a primary arrhythmia (SVT/AF), thyrotoxicosis or (rarely) phaeochromocytoma cannot be definitively excluded remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Recognise that a strictly posture-dependent pattern with a normal supine heart rate in a patient with a known autonomic disorder makes a benign orthostatic flare much more likely; arrange prompt GP review with ECG and thyroid function to formally exclude the mimics, and safety-net firmly for the red flags — rather than either dismissing the presentation or reflexively escalating it to ED.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: EITHER falsely reassuring the patient that it is 'definitely just POTS/anxiety' and omitting safety-netting, OR over-escalating every posture-dependent flare to 000/ED on the grounds that the mimics 'cannot be excluded remotely'.
